name: Crossing Portugal from Linarejos — Circular in the Comarca de Sanabria
name_original: Cruzando Portugal desde Linarejos — Circular en la Comarca de Sanabria
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Manzanal De Arriba traverse a diverse landscape within the Sierra de la Culebra, characterized by extensive forests of oak, chestnut, and pine. The region features varied terrain with paths and roads winding through hills and valleys, offering scenic routes. The Valparaíso Reservoir, formed by the Tera River, provides additional routes around its perimeter. Clear streams and rivers, such as the Onor, contribute to a lush natural environment. Best touring cycling routes around Manzanal De Arriba The most popular touring cycling route is Camino Sanabrés and Cernadilla Reservoir Loop, a 32.1 miles (51.7 km) trail that takes 3 hours 56 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers views of the Cernadilla Reservoir and varied terrain. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Crossing Portugal from Linarejos — Circular in the Comarca de Sanabria, a difficult 30.9 miles (49.7 km) path. This route features significant elevation gain and explores the Comarca de Sanabria region. Local touring cyclists also love the Camino Sanabrés and Muelas de los Caballeros Loop, a 36.6 miles (58.9 km) trail leading through the historic Camino Sanabrés, often completed in about 4 hours 30 minutes. Touring cycling around Manzanal De Arriba is defined by the forested hills of the Sierra de la Culebra, the Valparaíso Reservoir, and picturesque villages. The network offers options for different ability levels, though many routes present a significant challenge. The routes in Manzanal De Arriba are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from 2 reviews. More than 30 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Manzanal De Arriba's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in Manzanal De Arriba?
A: There are around 14 touring cycling routes in Manzanal De Arriba, offering diverse experiences through the Sierra de la Culebra. While many routes present a significant challenge, there are also options for moderate difficulty.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Manzanal De Arriba?
A: The region offers a beautiful cycling experience throughout the year. Spring and autumn are particularly pleasant, with mild temperatures and the forests displaying vibrant colors. The changing colors of the oak, chestnut, and pine forests provide a spectacular backdrop during these seasons.

Q: What are the typical difficulty levels for touring cycling routes in this area?
A: Touring cycling in Manzanal De Arriba is generally challenging. Out of the 14 available routes, 11 are classified as difficult, and 3 are moderate. There are currently no easy routes listed, indicating that most trails involve significant elevation changes and varied terrain.

Q: What natural features can I expect to see while cycling in Manzanal De Arriba?
A: You'll cycle through the extensive forests of oak, chestnut, and pine that characterize the Sierra de la Culebra. Many routes offer views of the Valparaíso Reservoir, formed by the Tera River, and you'll encounter crystal-clear streams and rivers like the Onor, which creates pleasant natural river beaches. You might also spot the impressive Peña Mira, the highest point in the Sierra de la Culebra.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural sites to visit along the touring cycling routes?
A: Yes, the region is rich in history and culture. You can explore traditional villages with stone houses, such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5539760" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Codesal&lt;/a&gt;, or visit the Honey Museum in Sagallos. Evidence of ancient human presence, including rock paintings, can be found, and a Roman road once connected Astorga and Braga through the area. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3866268" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of the Incarnation&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7173693" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Historic Stone Church&lt;/a&gt; are also notable religious buildings.

Q: What picturesque villages can I cycle through in the region?
A: Manzanal de Arriba encompasses several charming villages perfect for cycling through, including Codesal, Folgoso de la Carballeda, Linarejos, Pedroso de la Carballeda, Sagallos, Sandín, and Santa Cruz de los Cuérragos. &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1679368" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rio de Onor Village&lt;/a&gt; is particularly picturesque, known for its magical views from its bridge.

Q: Are there circular touring cycling routes available?
A: Yes, several routes are designed as loops. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1402714548/camino-sanabres-and-cernadilla-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camino Sanabrés and Cernadilla Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1404596478/camino-sanabrés-and-muelas-de-los-caballeros-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camino Sanabrés and Muelas de los Caballeros Loop&lt;/a&gt; offer circular journeys. Another option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1404049437/crossing-portugal-from-linarejos-circular-in-the-comarca-de-sanabria" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Crossing Portugal from Linarejos — Circular in the Comarca de Sanabria&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any viewpoints or scenic spots along the routes?
A: Absolutely. The routes often wind through hills and valleys, providing scenic vistas of the Sierra de la Culebra. The Valparaíso Reservoir offers beautiful waterside views, and you can find specific viewpoints like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7370662" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Rolling Hills in Montesinho Natural Park&lt;/a&gt;, which is accessible from some routes.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy the most about touring cycling in Manzanal De Arriba?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Cyclists often praise the diverse landscapes, the tranquility of the forests, and the opportunity to explore traditional rural life in the picturesque villages.

Q: Are there any specific natural monuments to look out for?
A: Yes, the region features several natural monuments. You can visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5814010" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Weir and Boardwalk of the Rio Onor&lt;/a&gt;, explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6014548" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Carbón Stream and Antigua Losera de Figueruela&lt;/a&gt;, or marvel at the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7372143" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Centennial Pyrenean Oak (Carvalho-Negral)&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any touring cycling routes that pass by castles or historic towns?
A: While Manzanal de Arriba itself is known for its traditional villages, you can find routes that lead to nearby historic towns. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38158041" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puebla de Sanabria Castle – Old Town of Puebla de Sanabria loop from Asturianos&lt;/a&gt; offers a chance to explore the historic castle and old town of Puebla de Sanabria.
tips: Beautiful views of the Sanabria region and the Culebra mountain range.
Picturesque town on the banks of the Onar river. The views from the Ponte de Rio de Onor are magical.
A small town in the Aliste region, Riomanzanas is located a few kilometers from the Sierra de la Culebra. The town is close to the border with Portugal.
Linarejos is a picturesque town near the border with Portugal. Among its houses many traditional stone houses are preserved, some modernized. Here you can find rural houses and even a bar.

waypoints.name: View of Sanabria and Sierra de la Culebra
